behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model output: whether speaker diarization/labels are preserved correctly. A stubbed STT response would hide this failure (MOCK-HIDE). REAL-INFRA may be present, but model-output correctness is the key dependency (LLM-DEP/ML-DEP), so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets a single model outcome ('speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels'), not multi-step agentic correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real audio with multiple speakers through the real STT API and assert transcript includes stable speaker-labeled utterances sufficient to attribute statements by attendee.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser logic over transcript text to structured fields; the failure can be caught inside one local parsing boundary with no I/O (MOCK-MISS says Unit floor applies). Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module state propagation, only extraction into an input structure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript strings into the parsing function and assert extracted assignments contain correct assignee and task text pairs in the expected structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real summarizer model behavior: coverage of assigned attendees in generated action items. Deterministic fixtures can validate orchestration but cannot verify model decision quality (deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected Workflow: behavior text checks one output constraint of the live summary, not a required sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcript content with known attendee-task assignments to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior wording scopes to routing logic after decisions are already identified ('identified decisions'), so minimum failure boundary is local mapping/schema placement logic. Rejected Agentic: text does not require validating model decision identification quality. Rejected Integration: no explicit cross-module handoff requirement beyond local routing.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision objects, test routing/mapping function places them in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates real model extraction/placement quality from transcript to structured summary. A mock summary would always pass and hide real misses (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Integration by INTEGRATION-BRAKE(b): correctness depends on AI output content.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript inputs containing explicit decisions through the live summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing (split -> process segments -> merge) with correctness depending on combined segment outputs and coherence across steps (MULTI-STEP). This is a chained agent pipeline boundary. Rejected Agentic: single-step model validation is insufficient because failure can occur in inter-step ordering/merge across multiple segment runs.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute production segmentation and sequential processing path, then assert all segments were processed in order and merged output is one coherent structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly end-to-end across multiple stages including real transcription and summarization, i.e., multiple agentic steps plus system boundaries. Minimum real failure boundary is full workflow execution. Rejected System/Agentic: each alone misses multi-stage handoff/orchestration failures stated by 'full pipeline ... without manual intervention'.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real meeting recording through production upload, transcription, summarization, and delivery path; assert automatic completion and valid final structured output with expected sections.
